intro: Statistical foundations: convergence modes, limit theorems, concentration inequalities, and minimax lower-bound tools.

namespace_intros.Bootstrap: Bootstrap foundations: resampling distributions and consistency of bootstrap approximations.
namespace_intros.CLT: Central limit theory: iid and triangular-array CLTs, Lindeberg conditions, and vector-valued versions used by the estimation layer.
namespace_intros.Concentration: Concentration inequalities: Hoeffding/Bernstein-type bounds, bounded differences, McDiarmid, symmetrization, and empirical-Bernstein refinements.
namespace_intros.EmpiricalProcess: Empirical-process theory: Rademacher complexities, maximal inequalities, moduli of continuity, and uniform laws for function classes.
namespace_intros.EmpiricalProcess.Equicontinuity: Stochastic equicontinuity: asymptotic-equicontinuity criteria for empirical processes.
namespace_intros.GMM: Generalized method of moments: identification, consistency, and asymptotic normality of GMM estimators.
namespace_intros.Inference: Inferential tooling: delta methods (scalar, functional, Hadamard), Wald/chi-squared statistics, and Slutsky ingredients.
namespace_intros.Limit: Convergence concepts: convergence in probability and distribution, continuous mapping, and Slutsky-style composition rules.
namespace_intros.MEstimation: M- and Z-estimation: consistency, asymptotic normality, and influence-function representations.
namespace_intros.Minimax: Minimax lower-bound machinery: risk definitions, Le Cam two-point methods, Pinsker/KL bridges, and rate statements for estimation problems.
namespace_intros.Nonparametric: Nonparametric methods: approximation theory, weighted least squares, local-polynomial and series/sieve estimators, higher-order influence functions, and moment-problem tools.
namespace_intros.Nonparametric.MomentProblems: Moment-problem substrate for nonparametric statistics: raw-moment algebra, L² projection residuals, constrained score programs, and sharp bounded-outcome residual envelopes.
namespace_intros.Nonparametric.MomentProblems.ResidualQua: Residual-quadratic moment problems: moment-coordinate formulas, measure-level L² projection residuals, and projection residual orthogonality identities.
namespace_intros.Nonparametric.MomentProblems.ScoreProgra: Dual score-program tools for moment problems: projection residual functions, feasible score directions, quadratic objectives, and minimum-norm identities.
namespace_intros.Nonparametric.MomentProblems.BoundedOutc: Sharp bounded-outcome residual envelopes: quartic root selection, admissible laws, upper bounds, and extremal three-point attainment.
namespace_intros.Orthogonality: Neyman orthogonality as a statistical property: definitions and the second-order remainder characterization.
namespace_intros.Quantile: Quantile processes: empirical quantiles, Bahadur representations, and joint sample-quantile asymptotics.
namespace_intros.Quantile.SampleQuantileBahadur: The Bahadur representation of sample quantiles: linearization, oscillation control, and the rate statement.
namespace_intros.Sample: Sampling primitives: iid samples as measurable maps with their product-law identities.
namespace_intros.SampleSplit: Sample splitting and cross-fitting: fold structures, split σ-algebras, and the independence facts they deliver.
namespace_intros.UStatistic: U-statistics: Hoeffding decompositions and asymptotics for degenerate and non-degenerate kernels.
namespace_intros.UStatistic.OrderM: Fixed-order m U-statistics: ordered injective tuples, Hájek projections, variance identities, degeneracy conditions, remainder negligibility, CLTs, and the order-2 bridge.
namespace_intros.Concentration.Matrix: Matrix concentration for random design moment matrices: iid entrywise deviation bounds, deterministic inverse perturbation, and high-probability control of the design inverse.
namespace_intros.PolynomialTail: Polynomial lower-tail moment analysis: the polynomial small-value tail law for a bounded positive variable, truncated inverse first/second moment asymptotics, the trimming-bias bound, and the bias-variance rate-balancing algebra.
